records.ety: [OF. despense, F. dpense, buffet, buttery, fr. OF. despendre to spend, distribute, L. dispendere, dispensum. See Dispense, Spend.]

defs: A place where provisions are kept; a buttery; a larder; a pantry.
quotes: In . . . his spence, or “pantry” were hung the carcasses of a sheep or ewe, and two cows lately slaughtered.
Bluff Harry broke into the spence, And turned the cowls adrift.

defs: The inner apartment of a country house; also, the place where the family sit and eat.
